attacking_style: Launches final attacks within 600m-6km to go from established breakaways, preferring late decisive accelerations after conserving energy. Targets medium-length climbs (20km at 7%) where breakaway sprinting ability becomes decisive.
team_tactics: Minimal team support visible; relies on solo efforts from breakaways rather than team-led setups. Operates independently, conserving energy within break groups before late acceleration.
terrain_preference: Medium-gradient sustained climbs (7%) of 20km where breakaway dynamics favor patient, strong finishers. Thrives when sprint speed can be deployed after attrition has reduced competition.
vulnerability: Requires breakaway establishment to be effective; no evidence of success from peloton or on explosive/steep gradients. Age (31+ during observations) suggests declining recovery between efforts.
key_patterns: Attacks from established breakaways in final 6km, not from peloton (2 instances)
Patient energy conservation before late acceleration, described as 'hoarding energy' tactics (2 instances)
Targets visibility and results through aggressive breakaway participation, contract-motivated riding (2 instances)
Operates as lone ranger without team support in decisive moments (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: 3-year gap between observations shows opportunistic wins when breakaways succeed, but no clear progression pattern
